patient_instruction_or_setup: Cover one eye and read the smallest line possible; use reading glasses if applicable; repeat other eye

suggested_options: Baseline / Reduced right / Reduced left / Reduced both / Unable
include_when: Vision complaint; pituitary or optic pathway disease; stroke; neuro exam

patient_instruction_or_setup: Cover one eye; look at examiner's nose; report number of fingers
examiner_technique: Examiner covers opposite eye; hands 1-2 feet from patient, equidistant; test 4 quadrants each eye with 1, 2, or 5 fingers

suggested_options: Full / Deficit right / Deficit left / Deficit bilateral / Unable

patient_instruction_or_setup: Follow examiner finger with eyes while keeping head still

examiner_technique: Dim lights; shine light into each eye twice; observe direct and consensual response; do not do same eye twice

suggested_options: Symmetric / Diminished right / Diminished left / Absent / Unable

patient_instruction_or_setup: Tell examiner whether sensation feels sharp and same or similar

examiner_technique: Place fingers on masseter muscles

suggested_options: Symmetric / Weak right / Weak left / Unable

patient_instruction_or_setup: Close eyes tightly; resist examiner opening them; then open eyes widely
examiner_technique: Try to open closed eyelids; observe wide eye opening

examiner_technique: Observe facial symmetry

examiner_technique: Use light source; observe palate and uvula

Strike patellar tendon below patella; test bilaterally

examiner_technique: Interpret Rinne result

function_or_clinical_use: Air conduction benefits from tympanic membrane and ossicle amplification and broader frequency range
suggested_options: Normal AC > BC / Conductive pattern BC >= AC / Unable

examiner_technique: Interpret Rinne result
observe_or_positive_finding: Bone conduction greater or equal air conduction suggests conductive loss
function_or_clinical_use: Bone conduction bypasses conductive block such as wax, fluid, or ossicle problem
suggested_options: Absent / Present right / Present left / Present bilateral

examiner_technique: Interpret Weber result
function_or_clinical_use: Skull vibration reaches both cochleae equally by bone conduction

examiner_technique: Interpret Weber result
observe_or_positive_finding: Sound lateralizes to bad ear in conductive loss
function_or_clinical_use: Less ambient sound and altered tympanic membrane tension can make bone-conducted sound clearer
suggested_options: Possible right conductive / Possible left conductive / Not suggested

examiner_technique: Interpret Weber result
observe_or_positive_finding: Sound lateralizes to good ear in sensorineural loss
function_or_clinical_use: Damaged cochlea or auditory nerve detects both air and bone conduction poorly
suggested_options: Possible right sensorineural / Possible left sensorineural / Not suggested
